title: Soul divisibility in NME
wikitext: []: Soul divisibility in NME             Quote Post by boyfrand » Fri Mar 30, 2012 9:41 pm Hello! Please direct me to a thread on this if it already exists, perhaps I overlooked it in my search. Are there any clues, or implicit clues that indicate that souls are indeed still divisible in NME? Rei's personality is more active/discernible in NME (such as not letting Asuka slap her in the elevator scene), is it possibly due to her having her complete soul? Perhaps her personality is different due to the fact that it is unclear whether she is Adam or Lilith this time, BUT it can be noted that Kaworu had a complete soul in NGE, and his personality was much more discernible/active than Rei's personality through much of the series (don't bother to respond if your response is "This is NME, it's not binded by events in NGE blah blah blah," I know). Also, in NGE, I had the impression that she did not retain her fond feelings for Gendo when she became fond of Shinji, however, in NME she wishes to bring them together. I haven't rewatched 2.22 recently, are there any clues about what is going on with the soul/core for Asuka's evas? Is it still her mother? Is she aware (in any way) that there is a soul in the core? Semi-related: I did rewatch 1.11 recently, there's no explanation of the MAGI's three parts. Is this explained in 2.22 and I can't remember? I believe in the original, soul divisibility was analogous to the way Naoko technologically divided her soul into three parts. I'm not implying that the concepts hold true in NME, I'm pointing out why I noticed that the explanation of the three different MAGI computers was missing. Anyway, does anyone have more information on soul divisibility in NME? Well, inversely, is soul combination possible?! At the end of 2.0, was Rei simply joining Shinji in 01 or were their souls perhaps combining?! Actually, to argue against my point, Big Z combined with Rei and 00 only physically, apparently Rei herself was still floating around somewhere inside Big Z, whether it was physically or just her soul.

Article: Soul divisibility in NME
[]: Soul divisibility in NME             Quote Post by boyfrand » Fri Mar 30, 2012 9:41 pm Hello! Please direct me to a thread on this if it already exists, perhaps I overlooked it in my search. Are there any clues, or implicit clues that indicate that souls are indeed still divisible in NME? Rei's personality is more active/discernible in NME (such as not letting Asuka slap her in the elevator scene), is it possibly due to her having her complete soul? Perhaps her personality is different due to the fact that it is unclear whether she is Adam or Lilith this time, BUT it can be noted that Kaworu had a complete soul in NGE, and his personality was much more discernible/active than Rei's personality through much of the series (don't bother to respond if your response is "This is NME, it's not binded by events in NGE blah blah blah," I know). Also, in NGE, I had the impression that she did not retain her fond feelings for Gendo when she became fond of Shinji, however, in NME she wishes to bring them together. I haven't rewatched 2.22 recently, are there any clues about what is going on with the soul/core for Asuka's evas? Is it still her mother? Is she aware (in any way) that there is a soul in the core? Semi-related: I did rewatch 1.11 recently, there's no explanation of the MAGI's three parts. Is this explained in 2.22 and I can't remember? I believe in the original, soul divisibility was analogous to the way Naoko technologically divided her soul into three parts. I'm not implying that the concepts hold true in NME, I'm pointing out why I noticed that the explanation of the three different MAGI computers was missing. Anyway, does anyone have more information on soul divisibility in NME? Well, inversely, is soul combination possible?! At the end of 2.0, was Rei simply joining Shinji in 01 or were their souls perhaps combining?! Actually, to argue against my point, Big Z combined with Rei and 00 only physically, apparently Rei herself was still floating around somewhere inside Big Z, whether it was physically or just her soul.
